title: NPTEL IoT 2022 JULY

questions.question: Which of the following is a static mechanism for address allocation of IoT nodes?

options.text: IPv6 address based on a hardware identifier
Both configuration over a management interface & based on a hardware identifier
IoT nodes can't be configured statically

text: Both configuration over a management interface & based on a hardware identifier

questions.detailed_solution:
IoT nodes can be statically configured in different ways, such as an
address can be configured over a management interface, or a node may use IPv6 address
based on a hardware identifier.
questions.answer_display: Both configuration over a management interface & based on a hardware identifier

questions.question: Which of the following error is commonly found in heating of metal strips?
questions.detailed_solution:
A hysteresis error causes the sensor output value to vary depending on
the sensor's previous input values. It is typically found in analog sensors, magnetic sensors,
heating of metal strips.

questions.question: Identify the component shown below.
questions.detailed_solution:
Passive infrared (PIR) sensors use a pair of pyroelectric sensors to
detect infrared energy radiating from objects within their field of vision.

questions.question: "X" performs active application layer functions between IoT nodes and other entities. What is X?
questions.detailed_solution:
IoT proxy is an entity that performs an active application layer function
between IoT nodes, and other entities. The IoT proxy can be collocated with the IoT
gateway.

questions.question: Which of the following field in IPv4 header format indicates that it is concerned with reliable transmission?
questions.detailed_solution:
The IPv4 emphasizes more on reliable transmission, as is evident by
fields, such as type of service, total length, id, offset, TTL, checksum fields.

questions.question: Which of the following is a function of IoT Gateway?
questions.detailed_solution:
An IoT gateway performs several critical functions, like switching,
routing, translating protocols, firewall &VPN services, security, data forwarding, managing
device, and filtering data.

questions.question: Which of the following actuator converts pressure into force?
questions.detailed_solution:
Pneumatic actuators enable large forces to be produced from relatively
small pressure changes (e.g., Pneumatic brakes are very responsive to small changes in
pressure applied by the driver)

questions.question: Which of the following mechanism for address autoconfiguration provide more control on the allocated addresses?
questions.detailed_solution:
DHCPv6 can be used to explicitly configure IPv6 addresses to nodes,
thereby providing network administrators with added control over the nodes on their
networks.

questions.question: Which of the following can measure position?
questions.detailed_solution:
A potentiometer sensor measures the distance or displacement of an
object in a linear or rotary motion and converts it into an electrical signal. An encoder is an
electromechanical device that can measure motion or position.

questions.question: In a scenario when the IoT proxy has uplink connectivity, does the IoT LAN need global addressing mandatorily?
questions.detailed_solution:
When the IoT proxy has uplink connectivity, it proxies communication
between the local IoT nodes and nodes in the external network. In this scenario, where all
communications go through a proxy, the IoT LAN does not need global addressing, but can
manage with link-local or ULA addresses, depending on the type of proxy.

questions.question: Which of the following is TRUE?

options.text: M2M is not a subset of IoT

questions.detailed_solution:
M2M is a part of the IoT, where IoT has a broader scope than M2M.
Both are different since M2M uses point-to-point communication. IoT and WoT aren't
same.

questions.question: Statement I: Aliasing error occurs if the input variable or added noise changes periodically at a frequency proportional to the multiple of the sampling rate. Statement II: If the signal is monitored digitally, the sampling frequency can cause a dynamic error triggering aliasing error.

options.text: Both Statement I and II are false
Both Statement I and II are true
Statement I is false but Statement II is true
Statement I is true but Statement II is false

text: Both Statement I and II are true

questions.detailed_solution:
If the signal is monitored digitally, the sampling frequency can cause
a dynamic error, or if the input variable or added noise changes periodically at a frequency
proportional to the multiple of the sampling rate, aliasing errors may occur.
questions.answer_display: Both Statement I and II are true

questions.question: Does polymer-based actuator find application in manufacturing artificial muscles?
questions.detailed_solution:
Shape memory polymer (SMP) actuators function similar to our
muscles, even providing a response to a range of stimuli such as light, electrical, magnetic,
heat, pH, and moisture changes.

questions.question: Unique Local Addresses are intended to allow routing over a network that expands over multiple links and routing hops, and even can expand over multiple networks. Can these addresses prevent address collision?
questions.detailed_solution:
Unique Local Addresses are designed to be used in local networks
larger than a single link, but not for communications through the Internet. However, these
are designed to provide adequate uniqueness in order to have extremely small risk of
address collision.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of vector sensor?
questions.detailed_solution:
Physical quantities such as sound, image, velocity, acceleration,
orientation, etc. are all vector quantities, as only their magnitude is not sufficient to convey
the complete information.

questions.question: Which of the following protocol is used to implement device management for mobile devices?
questions.detailed_solution:
OMA-DM is a protocol specified by the Open Mobile Alliance and is
often used in the context of complex structured mobile solutions. It is often preferred by
service providers to implement device management for mobile devices.

questions.question: In LOADng, can intermediate routers respond to Route Requests (RREQs)?

options.text: Yes, only when RREQ flooding occurs
Yes, only if they have active routes to the sought destination

questions.detailed_solution:
Intermediate LOADng Routers are explicitly prohibited from
responding to RREQs, even if they may have active routes to the sought destination.

questions.question: Which of the following functions as the network bridge in IEEE 802.15.4 network?
questions.detailed_solution:
Every IEEE 802.15.4 network has a minimum of one coordinator device
type who acts as the root; it also functions as the network bridge. The coordinator performs
data handling and storing operations.

questions.question: Which of the following allow communication between Zigbee and no-Zigbee devices?
questions.detailed_solution:
In order to enable communication between Zigbee and no-Zigbee
devices, a layer-7 application-level gateway has to be created, which is quite complex.
Refer to the book: S. Misra, A. Mukherjee, and A. Roy, 2020. Introduction to IoT.
Cambridge University Press.

questions.question: Statement I: RFIDs are radio-frequency-based. Statement II: RFID devices can act as both readers as well as tags. Which one of the following is correct?

options.text: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Statement I is false but Statement II is true

text: Statement I is true but Statement II is false

questions.detailed_solution:
RFIDs are primarily radio-frequency-based, which can work even when
they are not visible. These devices can't act as both readers as well as tags.
questions.answer_display: Statement I is true but Statement II is false

questions.question: In AMQP, which of the following functionality defines its component "exchange"?

options.text: defines rules for message routing to various queues
routing the messages to various queues

text: routing the messages to various queues

questions.detailed_solution:
Messages are not posted directly in the queue; rather, the user sends
messages to the exchange. An exchange is responsible for routing the messages to the
various queues.
questions.answer_display: routing the messages to various queues

questions.question: Which of the following is designed to have low overhead and better scalability in terms of dense networks?
questions.detailed_solution:
RPL is designed to have low overhead and better scalability in terms of dense
networks.

questions.question: Which of the following AMQP frame type controls the message flow rate?
questions.detailed_solution: "Flow" frame type controls the message flow rate.

questions.question: Does 6LoWPAN allow interoperability between IEEE802.15.4-based wireless devices and other IP-based devices?
questions.detailed_solution:
6LoWPAN allows interoperability between IEEE802.15.4-based
wireless devices, as well as other IP-based devices. 6LoWPAN simply uses a bridge to
enable communication between other devices.
Refer to the book: S. Misra, A. Mukherjee, and A. Roy, 2020. Introduction to IoT.
Cambridge University Press.

questions.question: In the context of XMPP, which of the following correctly defines BOSH?

options.text: Both XML streaming and HTTP binding

questions.detailed_solution:
BOSH is the HTTP binding for XMPP communications that is intended
to be used in situations where a device or client is unable to maintain a long-lived TCP
connection to an XMPP server.

questions.question: Does MQTT support event-driven architecture?
questions.detailed_solution:
MQTT protocol uses a publish/subscribe architecture. Publish/subscribe
is event‐driven and enables messages to be pushed to clients.

questions.question: Which of the following is the acronym for "Extensible Messaging and Presence Protocol"?
questions.detailed_solution: XMPP stands for Extensible Messaging and Presence Protocol.

questions.question: An IoT network requires communication mechanism, which is capable of synchronous as well as asynchronous communication. The mechanism should support both request-response, as well as publish-subscribe models. Which of the following protocol would be best suited for such a mechanism?
questions.detailed_solution:
CoAP is capable of synchronous as well as asynchronous
communication. It supports both request-response as well as publish-subscribe models.
Refer to the book: S. Misra, A. Mukherjee, and A. Roy, 2020. Introduction to IoT.
Cambridge University Press.

questions.question: Which of the following technique is used to transmit data frames in Beacon-enabled IEEE
questions.detailed_solution:
The periodic transmission of beacon messages characterizes beacon
enabled networks. Here, the data frames are sent via slotted CSMA/CA with a super-frame
structure managed by a personal area network (PAN) coordinator.

questions.question: What is the maximum size of hop limit in 6LoWPAN packet format?
questions.detailed_solution:
Hop limit in 6LoWPAN packet format allows up to 8-bit unsigned
integer. Decremented by 1 by each node that forwards the packet. The packet is discarded
if Hop Limit is decremented to zero.

questions.question: Which of the following handles packets, paging and inquiry in Bluetooth?
questions.detailed_solution: Bluetooth baseband handles packets, paging and inquiry.

questions.question: Which of the following incorporates channel hopping after every packet transmission?
questions.detailed_solution:
The major difference between WirelessHART and Zigbee is the
incorporation of channel hopping after every packet transmission in WirelessHART. A
Zigbee typically does not have hopping in it, but some variants of Zigbee hops channels
only when the entire network hops the channel.

questions.question: In "X" protocol, a central network controller device sets‐up a network with 1 Network ID and multiple node IDs for the devices in it. The nodes with different Network IDs cannot communicate with each other. What is "X"?
questions.detailed_solution:
"X" is Z-wave. In Z-wave, nodes with different Network IDs cannot
communicate with each other.

questions.question: In the context of Sensor Web, SensorML stands for _____________.
questions.detailed_solution: SensorML stands for Sensor Modeling language.

questions.question: Which of the following protocol is best suited to provide interference-free communication while minimizing latency and ensuring quality of service?
questions.detailed_solution:
Both wirelessHART and ISA100.11A have mechanisms to tackle
channel interference. However, ISA100.11A provides support for legacy technology
integration, as well as, minimizes latency and ensures quality of service. These features are
not explicitly present in wirelessHART. In light of these additional features, ISA100.11A is
the best suited option.
Refer to the book: S. Misra, A. Mukherjee, and A. Roy, 2020. Introduction to IoT.
Cambridge University Press.

questions.question: Which of the following solution approach is used to overcome the limitation of ineffective wakeup and sensing under rare event monitoring scenario in social sensing?
questions.detailed_solution:
Social sensing in WSNs often face the limitation of incapability of
distinguishing the rare events from regular events and ineffective wakeup and sensing under
rare event monitoring scenario. These are usually overcome by duty-cycle management.

questions.question: Which of the following technique Bluetooth master uses to avoid collisions during transmission in a Piconet?
questions.detailed_solution:
In a Piconet, the Bluetooth master is responsible for transmission
control by dividing the network into a series of time slots amongst the network members, as
a part of time division multiplexing scheme.

questions.question: Which of the following limitation is faced by total co-operation in Wireless Ad Hoc and Sensor Networks?
questions.detailed_solution:
In total co-operation, if all relay requests are accepted, nodes will
quickly exhaust limited energy.

questions.question: Which of the following can function over a reliable packet transfer protocol?
questions.detailed_solution:
Service Discovery Protocol (SDP) in Bluetooth can function over a
reliable packet transfer protocol.

questions.question: In which of the following mode, NFC devices can act as both readers as well as tags?

options.text: NFC devices can't act as both readers as well as tags

questions.detailed_solution:
NFC devices can act as both readers as well as tags (only in emulation
mode).

questions.question: In WSN, Information theoretic self‐management (INTSEM) controls the transmission rate of a node by adjusting a node's ____________.
questions.detailed_solution:
Information theoretic self‐management (INTSEM) controls the
transmission rate of a node by adjusting a node's sleep time.

questions.question: Which of the following layer of ISA 100.11A supports mesh routing?
questions.detailed_solution:
Data link layer of ISA 100.11A supports mesh routing and frequency
hopping.

questions.question: Which of the following is used to avoid interference-prone channels in HART?
questions.detailed_solution:
In HART, interference‐prone channels are avoided by using channel
switching post every transmission.

questions.question: When the devices are not in range, messages are routed through different nodes to bypass obstructions created by household appliances or layouts. Which of the following exhibit such characteristic of avoiding radio dead-spots?
questions.detailed_solution:
The process of bypassing radio dead‐spots is done using a message
called Healing. Healing messages are a characteristic of Z-wave.

questions.question: Which of the following is TRUE in terms of power (in mW) and range (in m) of the classes of Bluetooth devices?
questions.detailed_solution:
Class-1: Power=100mW, Range=100m; Class-2: Power=2.5 mW,
Range=10m

questions.question: Which of the following UAV topology is self-configuring?
questions.detailed_solution:
Star topology is not self-configuring, while mesh topology is self-
configuring.

questions.question: In WMSNs application, which of the following deployment can provide better sensing and prolong network lifetime?

options.text: Both scalar and camera sensor nodes

text: Both scalar and camera sensor nodes

questions.detailed_solution:
In WMSNs application, deployment of both camera sensor and scalar
sensor nodes can provide better sensing and prolong network lifetime. Camera sensor
questions.answer_display: Both scalar and camera sensor nodes

questions.question: In which of the following algorithms only a subset of nodes in the WASN participate in sensing, communication, and computation?
questions.detailed_solution:
Localized algorithms are a special type of distributed algorithms where
only a subset of nodes in the WASN participate in sensing, communication, and
computation.

questions.question: In general, the target-tracking problem consists of a WSN, whose nodes are strategically or randomly deployed across the sensor field. Is the statement true?
questions.detailed_solution:
According to several authors, the target-tracking problem consists of a
WSN, whose nodes are strategically or randomly deployed across the sensor field.
questions.reference: See lecture 16 [Research Paper: Souza, Éfren L., Eduardo F. Nakamura, and Richard W. Pazzi. "Target tracking for sensor networks: A survey." ACM Computing Surveys (CSUR) 49, no. 2 (2016): 1-31.]

questions.question: In Ad-Hoc FANETs, which of the following set-up can be considered as ground network?
questions.detailed_solution:
In Ad-Hoc FANETs, ground networks may be stationary WSNs or
VANETS or Control stations.

questions.question: Which of the following components in M2M monitors the status of devices and M2M area networks, and controls them based on their status?
questions.detailed_solution:
M2M device platform manages device profiles, such as location, device
type, address, and description. It monitors the status of devices and M2M area networks,
and controls them based on their status.

questions.question: In the guided target-tracking problem formulation, a tracker is added to the problem. The tracker follows the trajectory defined by beacon nodes to intercept the target. Which of the following objectives best define this tracking algorithm?

options.text: to fuse data about the target
to use the least amount of communication to reach the target
to reach the target in the shortest possible time

text: to reach the target in the shortest possible time

questions.detailed_solution:
The tracker can be a person or a guided vehicle that receives the
position of the target from the network so that it can move toward the target. The objective
is to reach the target in the shortest possible time.
questions.answer_display: to reach the target in the shortest possible time

questions.question: In gateway selection algorithm in FANETs, each UAV acquires the information of all UAVs within its ________ hop(s).
questions.detailed_solution:
In gateway selection algorithm in FANETs, gateway selection is
initiated by selection of the most stable node in the sub‐area. Each UAV acquires the
information of all UAVs within its 2 hops.

questions.question: A crossing is covered if it is in the _________ region of at least one node's coverage disk.
questions.detailed_solution:
Crossings are the intersection points between disk boundaries or
between monitored space boundary and disk boundaries. A crossing is covered if it is in the
interior region of at least one node's coverage disk.

questions.question: There are several major challenges related to target-tracking applications. Which of the following is NOT among those challenges?
questions.detailed_solution:
Authors identified six major challenges related to target-tracking
applications. These are target detection, target recovery, energy management, position
computation, node cooperation, and future-position estimation. Node cooperation is one of
the challenges, in which nodes cooperate and fuse data, reducing the number of messages.

questions.question: Which of the following components in M2M manages inquiry?
questions.detailed_solution:
M2M User Platform manages M2M service user profiles and provides
functionalities such as, user registration, modification, charging, and inquiry.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT regarded as one of the major scatters of underwater sound in Underwater Acoustic Sensor Networks?
questions.detailed_solution:
In a layered shallow oceanic region, the inclusion of the effect of
internal solitons on the performance of the network is important. Based on various
observations, it is proved that non-linear internal waves, i.e., Solitons are one of the
major scatters of underwater sound.

questions.question: In Optimal Geographical Density Control (OGDC) algorithm, a node _________ if its coverage area is completely covered.
questions.detailed_solution:
In OGDC, a node closest to the optimal location becomes active. A
node sleeps if its coverage area is completely covered.

questions.question: Which of the following elements of target-tracking algorithm can perform data fusion?
questions.detailed_solution:
A sink node is the intermediary between the network and application
user. It is a special node with high processing power and storage. It can perform data fusion.
questions.reference: See lecture 16 [Research Paper: Souza, Éfren L., Eduardo F. Nakamura, and Richard W. Pazzi. "Target tracking for sensor networks: A survey." ACM Computing Surveys (CSUR) 49, no. 2 (2016): 1-31.]

questions.question: Which of the following components of Mobile Wireless Sensor Networks (MWSN) moves in order to collect data from sensor nodes?

options.text: Both data mule and mobile sink

text: Both data mule and mobile sink

questions.detailed_solution:
Mobile Sink moves in order to collect data from sensor nodes. A data
mule also moves to collect the data from sensor nodes and goes to the sink to deliver the
collected data.
questions.answer_display: Both data mule and mobile sink

questions.question: Fill in the blank. UMB consists of __________.
questions.detailed_solution: UMB consists of UMB-Core (UMB-C) and UMB-Adaptor (UMB-A).

questions.question: Fill in the blank. UMB creates virtual maps among the __________ of all middleware home networks.
questions.detailed_solution:
UMB creates virtual maps among the physical devices of all the home
networks such as HAVI, Jini, LonWorks, and UPnP.

questions.question: Which of the following is the functionality of UMB adaptors?

options.text: Translate local middleware's message into global metadata's message
Translate global middleware's message into global metadata's message
Translate local middleware's message into local metadata's message
Translate global middleware's message into local meta-data's message

text: Translate local middleware's message into global metadata's message

questions.detailed_solution:
UMB converts physical devices into virtually abstracted one and
translates local middleware's message into global metadata's message.
questions.answer_display: Translate local middleware's message into global metadata's message

questions.question: Which of the following is true?

options.text: Relay is an actuator that uses electromagnetic effect to act as a switch
Relay can open and close circuit when electricity is passed through it
Relay can be used to control the power supply to any other connected device

questions.detailed_solution:
From the basics of relay interface with NodeMCU.
Refer to the book: S. Misra, A. Mukherjee, and A. Roy, 2020. Introduction to IoT.
Cambridge University Press.

questions.question: What is sketch?
questions.detailed_solution:
Sketch is the program coded in Arduino IDE which mainly consists
of two parts setup() and loop.

questions.question: What is function setup()?

options.text: It iterates the task in the program

questions.detailed_solution: As per the basics of Arduino programming.

questions.question: Which of the following is true for the given command?

options.text: Gives random number within the range [0, 10]
Gives random number within the range [1, 10]
Gives random number within the range [0, 9]
Reset the pseudo-random number generator with seed value 10

text: Gives random number within the range [0, 10]

questions.detailed_solution: As per the basics of Arduino programming.
questions.answer_display: Gives random number within the range [0, 10]

questions.question: Which kind of conflict occur when different processing logics are applied to same IoT networked devices or applications?
questions.detailed_solution:
Semantic conflict occurs when different processing logics are applied
to same IoT networked devices or applications.

questions.question: Which of the following is TRUE for the sketch command given below?

options.text: Provides a delay of 3000 nano seconds
Provides a delay of 3000 simulation time

questions.detailed_solution: As per the basics of Arduino programming.

questions.question: Which of the following solutions are not responsible for generating unique address?
questions.detailed_solution: As per the basics of device identification and categorization.

questions.question: How many digital I/O pins are there in Arduino Uno.
questions.detailed_solution:
From the basics of Arduino Uno.
Refer to the book: S. Misra, A. Mukherjee, and A. Roy, 2020. Introduction to IoT.
Cambridge University Press.

questions.question: Fill in the blank. The interoperability between devices and device user in term of message formats is called _________.
questions.detailed_solution:
The interoperability in terms of message formats is called syntactic
interoperability.

questions.question: Fill in the blank. __________ dynamically maps physical devices with different domains.
questions.detailed_solution:
Middleware technology dynamically maps physical devices with
different domains and based on the map, the devices can be discovered and controlled
remotely.

questions.question: Which of the following ontology utilize previous data to estimate what is going to happen?
questions.detailed_solution: As per the basics of semantic interoperability for device ineraction.

questions.question: Which component of UMB converts physical devices into virtually abstracted one, as described by Universal Device Template (UMB).
questions.detailed_solution: As per the definition of UMB-A.

questions.question: What is the value that is assigned to the variable f in the given piece of python code?
questions.detailed_solution: As per program output.
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INTRODUCTION TO PYTHON PROGRAMMING- I@ 09:07)

questions.question: What is the output of the following piece of python code?
questions.detailed_solution: As per program output.
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INTRODUCTION TO PYTHON PROGRAMMING- I@ 21:05)

questions.question: Fill in the blanks. Raspbian is a/n ___________
questions.detailed_solution:
Raspbian is a free operating system for Raspberry Pi (Please refer to
lecture INTRODUCTION TO RASPBERRY PI-I@ 12:14).

questions.question: What is the output of the following piece of Python code?
questions.detailed_solution: As per the basics of Python programming.
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INTRODUCTION TO PYTHON PROGRAMMING- I@ 11:01)

questions.question: Fill in the blanks. Raspberry Pi 3 Model B has a GPU support of _________
questions.detailed_solution: Raspberry Pi 3 Model B has a GPU support of 400 MHz video core IV.
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INTRODUCTION TO RASPBERRY PI-I @ 6:00)

questions.question: Which of the following represents the command used for rebooting Raspberry Pi?
questions.detailed_solution: As per the basics of Python programming.
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INTRODUCTION TO RASPBERRY PI-I @ 26:53)

questions.question: State whether true or false. It is not possible to return multiple values from a function in Python.
questions.detailed_solution:
In python programming it is possible to return multiple values. (Please
refer to lecture INTRODUCTION TO PYTHON PROGRAMMING- I @ 24:41)

questions.question: What is the data type of the variable ls in the following piece of Python code?
questions.detailed_solution: As per the basics of Python programming
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INTRODUCTION TO PYTHON PROGRAMMING- I @ 15:07).

questions.question: State whether true or false. A function in Python may or may not return a value.
questions.detailed_solution: As per the basics of Python programming
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INTRODUCTION TO PYTHON PROGRAMMING- I @ 19:38).

questions.question: What are the basic modes to open a file in python?
questions.detailed_solution: As per basics of Python programming
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INTRODUCTION TO PYTHON PROGRAMMING-II @ 2:58).

questions.question: What are the socket types that exist in Python based socket programming?
questions.detailed_solution:
SOCK_DGRAM and SOCK_STREAM are the socket types whereas
AF_UNIX and AF_INET are socket families
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INTRODUCTION TO PYTHON PROGRAMMING-II @ 23:57).

questions.question: Does Python support exception handling?
questions.detailed_solution: As per basics of Python programming.
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INTRODUCTION TO PYTHON PROGRAMMING-I @ 28:39).

questions.question: Which of the following must be used to terminate a loop and move to the next code after the loop?
questions.detailed_solution: As per basics of Python programming
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INTRODUCTION TO PYTHON PROGRAMMING-I @ 29:37).

questions.question: Select the option that does not represent a keyword in Python language?
questions.detailed_solution: integer is not a keyword (As per basics of Python programming).

questions.question: Raspberry Pi does not support any other language other than Python?
questions.detailed_solution:
The default programming language installed in Raspberry Pi include
Python, C , C++, JAVA, Scratch and Ruby
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INTRODUCTION TO PYTHON PROGRAMMING- I @ 22:26).

questions.question: You have an IoT temperature sensor that is sensing temperature from a particular location and sending the data over the network to a server situated far away in another city for storage. This is an example of remote data logging.
For a two-way communication between a client and server, i.e both the client and the server send data to each other, it is important that both of them knows about the following about each other

options.text: Only the IP address of server
Only the port number of client
Both the IP address and port number
Neither the IP address nor the port number

text: Both the IP address and port number

questions.answer_display: Both the IP address and port number

questions.question: If you are using Raspberry Pi (RPi) to connect standard IoT sensors (such as DHT temperature sensors), which among the following components of the Raspberry Pi do you use connect your Pi to your sensor for data transfer.

options.text: MicroSD card slot of the RPi
Ethernet LAN port of the RPi
General Purpose Input Output (GPIO) pins of the RPi

text: General Purpose Input Output (GPIO) pins of the RPi

questions.detailed_solution:
The GPIO pins of the RPi board is responsible for connecting the RPi to
any external sensor/actuator. Refer standard materials on RPi and
questions.answer_display: General Purpose Input Output (GPIO) pins of the RPi

questions.question: It is possible to connect a single DHT temperature sensor with a suitable number of connection wires with two Raspberry Pis acting as a client for the same sensor.
questions.detailed_solution:
You can connect any number of RPis to the same sensor, with each RPi
acting as a separate client, provided you have suitable number of connection wires.

questions.question: Which among the following denotes the correct connection order for a standard 4-PIN DHT sensor with the order of PIN numbers taken as 4,3,2,1 (from right to left)
questions.detailed_solution: This is the standard configuration for a 4-PIN DHT sensor.

questions.question: Using the matplotlib library in Python, you are plotting a graph of pressure values versus time, with pressure taken in the vertical axis and time taken in the horizontal axis. While writing the code to plot the graph, which among the following is the correct parameter (denoted by '??') that you can write within the function "ylabel(??)"
questions.detailed_solution:
The function ylabel() sets the name of the Y-axis, i.e the vertical axis of a
graph. Since we are plotting pressure in the Y-axis, thus we should label it appropriately.

questions.question: Lack of centralized control is a problem and limitation of traditional network architectures without Software Defined Networking (SDN) support
questions.detailed_solution:
Traditional networks do not have a central authority over them, therefore
many issues take time to get resolved with distributed algorithms.

questions.question: Detaching the physical hardware from the overlying software components, such as the Operating System (OS) and applications forms an important aspect of transitioning to SDN
Flow rules are stored within SDN switches that run the OpenFlow protocol in the following format.
questions.detailed_solution:
Flow rules are stored in tabular formats within the switches based on
which packets get routed.
questions.reference: Refer Lecture 33, discussions on Rule Placement

questions.question: Among the following, which is the most unlikely to be considered as a suitable candidate for a match- field for flow rules in SDN

options.text: Source port number of the packet
Destination IP address of the packet

questions.detailed_solution:
The temperature of the motherboard of a switch generally has no role in
deciding how a packet will be forwarded. Refer to discussions on Flow rules in

questions.question: In SDN, the central controller of one network can communicate with the central controller of another SDN network through which of the following directional APIs
questions.detailed_solution:
In SDN, East and West bound APIs are used for inter-controller
communication in the control plane.

questions.question: Suppose in SDN, there are two switches S1 and S2. The same packet P arrives in both the switches, S2 does not have the appropriate flow rule to forward this particular packet, whereas S1 has. Then with respect to the two switches, what is the correct action that is taken.

options.text: S1 sends PACKET_IN to controller, S2 forwards the packet
S2 sends PACKET_IN to controller, S1 forwards the packet
Both S1 and S2 send PACKET_IN to controller
Both S1 and S2 forward the packet

text: S2 sends PACKET_IN to controller, S1 forwards the packet

questions.detailed_solution:
If a switch does not have an appropriate flow rule to process an incoming
packet, it sends PACKET_IN message to the controller, whereas if the switch has the flow
rule, it simply forwards the packet as per the flow rule.
questions.answer_display: S2 sends PACKET_IN to controller, S1 forwards the packet

questions.question: No matter how big an SDN network becomes, we can never have more than one SDN controller.
questions.detailed_solution:
There is provision for splitting a single controller into multiple distributed
but connected controllers in tree/ring/mesh topologies to manage a very large network.

questions.question: With Software Defined IoT, it is possible to control the individual physical sensor and actuator nodes as well as rule placement of the backbone network remotely by suitable orchestration and software.
questions.detailed_solution:
SDIoT allows flexible control and management of the entire IoT network,
from the physical devices to the backbone network.
questions.reference: Refer Lecture 35, architecture of SDN for IoT.

questions.question: Soft-WSN that has been proposed as one of the solutions to Software Defined IoT achieves the following
questions.detailed_solution:
Soft-WSN that has been proposed by Bera et. Al (IEEE SJ'16) achieves
both device and topology management efficiently.

questions.question: The process of abstracting physical network hardware and resources from the network services and use cases are known as
ODIN is one of the proposed solutions towards Software Defined WSN and mobility management. As per the architecture of ODIN, which component of ODIN is placed on top of the controller.
questions.detailed_solution: ODIN Master is associated with the SDN controller.

questions.question: Shared pool of resources with easy configurability are important aspects of cloud computing.
questions.detailed_solution:
Cloud computing deals with easy access of configurability of shared pool
of computing resources over network.

questions.question: In cloud computing, the users have specific knowledge about the exact physical location of the computing hardware with respect to the cloud services.
questions.detailed_solution:
In cloud computing, the users have no knowledge about the backend
physical hardware orchestration and management.

questions.question: Suppose there are two organizations, A and B both of which provide cloud services. Any user can access the cloud of A free of cost whereas only employees of organization B can access the cloud of B. Also organization B has set up its cloud for only its own needs. With respect to the type of cloud infrastructure provided by A and B, which among the following is true

options.text: A: Private Cloud, B: Public Cloud
A: Public Cloud, B: Private Cloud
A: Public Cloud, B: Public Cloud
A: Private Cloud, B: Private Cloud

text: A: Public Cloud, B: Private Cloud

questions.detailed_solution:
Since anyone can access the cloud services of A, it is public, whereas as
only employees can access the cloud of B, the latter is a private cloud.
questions.answer_display: A: Public Cloud, B: Private Cloud

questions.question: Which among the following can be considered as a potential service model for cloud computing.
questions.detailed_solution: All of the given options are well defined cloud service models.
questions.reference: Refer Lecture 37 and 38, cloud service models.

questions.question: Which of the following component can be considered as optional for a sensor node?
questions.detailed_solution:
Location finding unit, such as GPS is an optional component for any
sensor node.

questions.question: You have bought a spreadsheet processing software in the form of a CD-ROM which you would need to install yourself on your computer to run and then manage. Is it an example of SaaS in cloud computing?
questions.detailed_solution:
Cloud computing service models allows you to have access to software
over the Internet without the need to install them on your local computer.
questions.reference: Refer lecture on cloud computing service models.

questions.question: Suppose two persons A and B both wish to access a particular SaaS feature from a cloud service provider through their respective personal computers. Both of them log into the cloud at the same time. Then which among the following is true.

options.text: Neither A nor B will be able to access the services
A will be able to access the services but not B
B will be able to access the services but not A
Both A and B will be able to access the services

text: Both A and B will be able to access the services

questions.detailed_solution:
Cloud services are provided in a many to one manner by the service
provider simultaneously. So both will be able to access.
questions.answer_display: Both A and B will be able to access the services

questions.question: Along with Accounting, which among the following is an essential component of cloud service management.
questions.detailed_solution:
Billing and Accounting are two important cloud service management
activities.

questions.question: Which among the following security paradigms is concerned with securing the in-transit data between the users and the cloud through the Internet.
questions.detailed_solution:
Network level security is concerned with the security of the in-transit data
through the network in cloud computing.

questions.question: "Cloud Security ensures that _____________ users can access their data after proper ____________________" Choose which among the following should be the correct order of words to be filled in the blanks.
questions.detailed_solution:
Cloud security ensures proper authorization and authentication of only
legitimate users. Refer 39 on Cloud security models.

questions.question: Which among the following should be protected with proper access control in cloud computing
questions.detailed_solution:
Access control should be incorporated in all of the given access points in
cloud security.

questions.question: Before launching your cloud services as a product to customers, it is always a good idea to test your cloud applications on a cloud simulator first in the pre-deployment phase.
questions.detailed_solution:
Testing your cloud applications on a simulator first in the pre-deployment
phase can help you to correct many errors before actually launching them.

questions.question: With respect to virtualization in cloud computing, which among the following can be considered as suitable virtualization paradigms.
questions.detailed_solution:
All of the given options can be considered for virtualization in cloud
architectures. Refer Chapter 10 of reference book 'Introduction to IoT' by Misra et. Al
(Cambridge University Press'20)

questions.question: The OpenStack cloud simulation framework provides an interactive Graphical User Interface (GUI)
questions.detailed_solution: OpenStack provides a very interactive and efficient GUI.

questions.question: Virtualized resources within the OpenStack simulator that you can define, set parameters of and deploy within OpenStack are also known as
questions.detailed_solution:
In OpenStack instances refer to the virtual resources that you define,
initialize and deploy.

questions.question: If you want to connect your OpenStack instance with the external public network (like the Internet), you would need to connect the following between your instance and the public network
questions.detailed_solution:
Your OpenStack instance is not connected by default to the public
network, you would need to initialize a router that makes the connection.

questions.question: Which among the following is NOT a part of a sensor node connected to a wireless sensor network.
questions.detailed_solution:
Each sensor in a WSN is equipped with Sensing unit, Processing unit and
Communication unit.

questions.question: Consider that you have two types of sensors, soil monitoring sensor for smart farming and temperature sensor for smart industry. The concept that the correct (right) type of sensor must be deployed only at their correct and appropriate physical location suitable as per their functionalities is known as
questions.detailed_solution:
The sensor nodes must always be deployed at their appropriate location.
i.e place which is suitable as per their functionalities.

questions.question: Sensor-as-a-Service (Se-aaS) is an exciting new concept that brings the service models of cloud computing to traditional IoT sensor networks. In this aspect, which among the following forms an essential component of a Se-aaS architecture.
questions.detailed_solution:
Sensor virtualization is the principal concept behind the architecture and
orchestration of Se-aaS. Refer Chapter 10 of reference book 'Introduction to IoT' by Misra et.
(Cambridge University Press'20) Al and

questions.question: Considering the end-to-end architecture of a WSN, from the physical sensor nodes right up to the users, sensor-cloud supports different entities to have ownership of the different layers and components simultaneously.
questions.detailed_solution:
Sensor Cloud supports distributed ownership of different components of
the WSN architecture by different entities.

questions.question: In sensor cloud architecture the Sensor Cloud Service Provider (SCSP) is logically situated at the following position of the architecture

options.text: In the same level as the physical sensors
In between the physical sensors and the user layer
In the same level as the users

text: In between the physical sensors and the user layer

questions.detailed_solution:
The SCSP is concerned with providing the cloud based services to the
virtual sensor layer, hence it sits in the middle of the physical sensors and the user application
layer.
questions.answer_display: In between the physical sensors and the user layer

questions.question: In sensor cloud, the association between virtual sensor instances and the corresponding physical sensors follows
questions.detailed_solution:
One virtual sensor can be associated with multiple physical sensors,
similarly one physical sensor can be associated with many virtual sensors. Hence the mapping
is many-many.

questions.question: Dynamic caching mechanism improves the flexibility and efficiency of sensor cloud.
questions.detailed_solution:
Caching can improve in better handling the data movement and user
requests in a sensor cloud.
questions.reference: Refer Lecture 43 on Dynamic and Adaptive Data Caching mechanism.

questions.question: With respect to the caching based architecture of a sensor cloud, the External Cache (EC) has a direct data connection with which among the following

options.text: Both physical sensors and Internal Cache (IC)

text: Both physical sensors and Internal Cache (IC)

questions.detailed_solution:
The external cache serves as the intermediate interconnection between the
physical sensors below and the Internal Cache (IC) above.
questions.answer_display: Both physical sensors and Internal Cache (IC)

questions.question: Data from an IoT device is transferred to cloud via a network, which is then processed at the cloud and then a response is sent back to the IoT device from the cloud after processing. The time it takes for one- way data transfer between the node and cloud is 10s and the data processing time at the cloud is 'x' seconds. It takes a total of 25s for the entire to and fro transfer of data between the sensor and cloud along with processing at the cloud. What is the value of x?
questions.detailed_solution:
Time taken for one-way data transfer between the node and cloud is 10s.
Total time taken for the data transfer is 25s. So 25=10+x+10 (transfer from node to
cloud+processing at cloud+transfer from cloud to node). Thus x=5s.

questions.question: There are two types of sensor data, A and B. A is time sensitive that is required to be processed immediately, while B is not time sensitive and can tolerate longer time for processing. As per the standard utilities of cloud and fog computing, which among the following options show the correct processing locations for A and B.
questions.detailed_solution:
Time sensitive data are processed at the fog layer while data that can
tolerate processing delay are processed at the cloud layer.
questions.reference: Refer Lecture 45, the working of Fog.

questions.question: There can be multiple fog nodes in between the physical sensor layer at the bottom and the cloud layer at the top.
questions.detailed_solution:
There can be multiple fog nodes in between the cloud layer and the
physical sensor nodes for efficient processing.

questions.question: Which among the following is/are a potential problem and challenge in fog computing?
questions.detailed_solution:
There are several key challenges in fog computing, some of which are the
options as given above. Refer challenges of Fog computing in

questions.question: Fill in the blank. In LoNWorks, each neuron chip has __________ memory types.
questions.detailed_solution:
Each neuron chip has three memory types available with it: 1) ROM,
2) RAM, and 3) EEPROM.
Refer to the book: S. Misra, A. Mukherjee, and A. Roy, 2020. Introduction to IoT.
Cambridge University Press.__

questions.question: V2X follows centralized architecture.
questions.detailed_solution:
V2X follows distributed architecture, where contents are distributed
over the network.

questions.question: Which of the following is not true about Content Centric Networking?

options.text: Focuses more on actual location than data
Hierarchical data is transmitted directly instead of being part of a conversation
Enables scalable and efficient data dissemination
Derived from Information Centric Networking architecture

text: Focuses more on actual location than data

questions.detailed_solution:
The content centric networking focuses on data than its actual
location.
questions.answer_display: Focuses more on actual location than data

questions.question: In VANET, link durations are long and easily scaled-up to include all the vehicles on the road?
questions.detailed_solution: Link durations are short due to the highly dynamic nature of VANETs.

questions.question: Which of the following is not true about Ad-hoc domain.

options.text: Composed of vehicles (OBUs) and road-side units (RSUs)
Vehicles and road-side units are mobile
Communication takes place through DSRC stack
Communication mode may be either V2V or V2I

text: Vehicles and road-side units are mobile

questions.detailed_solution:
The vehicles are mobile however, road-side units are static. (Please
refer
questions.answer_display: Vehicles and road-side units are mobile

questions.question: Fill in the blank. In infrastructure domain, RSUs are connected to internet by means of __________.
questions.detailed_solution: From the basics of Infrastructure Domain.

questions.question: Which of the following is the draft standard for Wireless Access in Vehicular Environments (WAVE)?
questions.detailed_solution: From the basics of IEEE 1609 Family.

questions.question: Inferences drawn from multiple sensor type data is qualitatively superior to single sensor type data.
questions.detailed_solution: From the basics of Data Fusion.

questions.question: Which of the following statements are not correct regarding Data Fusion?

options.text: Expose critical data sources and semantics

text: Expose critical data sources and semantics

questions.detailed_solution:
Data Fusion enables hiding of critical data sources and semantics
(useful in military applications, medical cases, etc.
questions.answer_display: Expose critical data sources and semantics

questions.question: What happens at signal level of data fusion?

options.text: Fusion of information at the imaging device level itself
Fusion of information at the sensor node with the local network itself
Fusion of information prior to decision making

text: Fusion of information at the sensor node with the local network itself

questions.detailed_solution: From the stages of Data fusion.
questions.answer_display: Fusion of information at the sensor node with the local network itself

questions.question: Which of the following is not a functional layer of smart parking?
questions.detailed_solution:
Smart parking has three functional layers: 1. Information Collection, 2.
System Deployment, and 3. Service dissemination.

questions.question: Fill in the blank. Solutions related to Smart Cities should have ________.
questions.detailed_solution:
The energy-efficient solutions in Smart Cities should have lightweight
protocols, scheduling optimization, a predictive model for energy consumption, a cloud-based
approach, a low power transceiver, and a cognitive management framework.

questions.question: Fill in the blank. In UPnP, device search and advertisements are __________ through HTTP over UDP over port
questions.detailed_solution:
In UPnP, device search and advertisements are multicast through HTTP
over UDP over port 1900 whereas responses are returned in unicast manner.
Refer to the book: S. Misra, A. Mukherjee, and A. Roy, 2020. Introduction to IoT.
Cambridge University Press.

questions.question: Fill in the blank. UPnP is ________ layer technology.
questions.detailed_solution:
Universal Plug and Play is an application layer technology which is
mainly web-based

questions.question: In Lonworks, every device includes a Neuron Chip.
questions.detailed_solution: From the basics of HAN Standards.

questions.question: State whether true or false. In a smart grid, monitoring and restoration are performed manually.
questions.detailed_solution:
In a traditional electrical grid, monitoring and restoration are performed
manually.
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture SMART GRID- I@1:37).

questions.question: Smart home uses the emerging smart grid technologies to satisfy which of the following?

options.text: Contribute to the smooth and efficient functioning of the electric grid

questions.detailed_solution:
Smart home uses the emerging smart grid technologies to save energy,
seek out the lowest rate, and contribute to the smooth and efficient functioning of the electric
grid
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture SMART GRID- I@27:12).

questions.question: What is/are the benefit/s of using a smart grid by customers?

options.text: Updated information on their energy usage in real-time
Disabling the smart appliances and smart devices from charging

text: Updated information on their energy usage in real-time

questions.detailed_solution:
For customers, the benefit of using a smart grid includes, updated
information on their energy usage in real-time, different pricing options, and enabling smart
appliances and other smart devices to be charged
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture SMART GRID- I@14:34).
questions.answer_display: Updated information on their energy usage in real-time

questions.question: What is the full form of NAN?
questions.detailed_solution:
The full form of NAN is Neighborhood Area Network (Please refer to
lecture SMART GRID- II@12:02).

questions.question: State whether true or false. In smart grid communication, the gateway acts as a link between the smart meter and the data aggregator unit.
questions.detailed_solution:
In smart grid communication, the gateway acts as a link between the smart
meter and the data aggregator unit.
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture SMART GRID- II @ 14:01)

questions.question: What is/are the vulnerability/vulnerabilities that may exist in the smart grid?
questions.detailed_solution:
The vulnerability attacks that may exist in the smart grid include physical
threats, availability, and dynamic system attacks.
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture SMART GRID- II @ 16:54)

questions.question: Does the Industrial Internet of things follow the "rip & replace" approach?
questions.detailed_solution: Industrial Internet of things follows the "wrap & re-use" approach.
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INDUSTRIAL INTERNET OF THINGS- I @ 6:12)

questions.question: Match the following: Sensor Purpose

options.text: 2nd Industrial revolution c. b. Mechanized production
3rd Industrial revolution d. c. Industrial Internet of Things
4th Industrial revolution d. Internet evolution and automation

questions.detailed_solution:
The industrial evolution of IIoT is as follows.
1. 1st Industrial revolution- Mechanized production
2. 2nd Industrial revolution- Mass production
3. 3rd Industrial revolution- Internet evolution and automation
4. 4th Industrial revolution- Industrial Internet of Things
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INDUSTRIAL INTERNET OF THINGS- I @ 10:12)

questions.question: The implanted on-body sensors on a patient's body have led to which of the following facility/facilities?

options.text: Better accuracy in the collection of data

questions.detailed_solution:
Patients can be continuously monitored due to the implanted on-body
sensors. This has led to - improved treatment outcomes, the cost has reduced, and improved
accuracy in the collection of data.
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INDUSTRIAL INTERNET OF THINGS- II @ 3:18)

questions.question: Which of the following is considered the primary challenges of IIoT?

options.text: Managing a huge amount of data

text: Managing a huge amount of data

questions.detailed_solution:
The primary challenges of IIoT includes the management of a huge
amount of data. Environmental protection falls under safety challenge and handling hazardous
substances is considered a hazard (related) challenge
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INDUSTRIAL INTERNET OF THINGS- II @ 15:27)
questions.answer_display: Managing a huge amount of data

questions.question: Which of the following is/are provided by the Intelligent Transport System?
questions.detailed_solution:
Intelligent Transport System provides vehicle-to-vehicle connectivity,
vehicle-to-sensor connectivity, and vehicle-to-internet connectivity.
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture INDUSTRIAL INTERNET OF THINGS- II @ 4:41)

questions.question: Fill in the blanks. Structured Query Language is mainly used for managing _____________ data in database.
questions.detailed_solution:
Structured Query Language is mainly used for managing structured data
in a database
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture DATA HANDLING AND ANALYTICS- I @9:06).

questions.question: What is/are the advantage/s of the application of ML on IoT?
questions.detailed_solution:
Some of the advantages of applying ML in IoT includes self-learner,
time-efficient, self-guided, and minimum human interaction requirement (Please refer to
book: S. Misra, A. Mukherjee, and A. Roy, 2020. Introduction to IoT. Cambridge
University Press. Chapter no.-17, page no.-357).

questions.question: Which of the following represent/s the veracity characteristics of Big data?
questions.detailed_solution:
Veracity (characteristics of big data) refers to noise, biases, and
abnormality in data
questions.reference: ( Please refer to lecture DATA HANDLING AND ANALYTICS- I @13:50).

questions.question: State whether true or false. NoSQL databases can be used for storing unstructured data.
questions.detailed_solution:
Querying languages such as NoSQL are generally used for unstructured
data. (Please refer to book: S. Misra, A. Mukherjee, and A. Roy, 2020. Introduction to IoT.
Cambridge University Press. Chapter no.-6, page no.-117).

questions.question: State whether true or false. In general, the data collected from an interview can be analyzed through qualitative analysis.
questions.detailed_solution:
In qualitative analysis, the data can be gathered by many methods like
interviews, videos, and audios
questions.reference: ( Please refer lecture Data Handling and Analytics- Part II @ 3:32).

questions.question: Which of the following are often involved with quantitative analysis?
questions.detailed_solution:
Analysis of variables, Precision, and Data dispersion are often involved
with quantitative analysis
questions.reference: ( Please refer lecture Data Handling and Analytics- Part II @ 4:41).

questions.question: State whether true or false. A complete model does not have the same number of variables as the number of equations.
questions.detailed_solution:
A complete model does have the number of variables equal to the number of
equations.
questions.reference: ( Please refer lecture Data Handling and Analytics- Part II @ 8:15)

questions.question: Which of the following model is mostly applied when it is necessary to compare more than 2 populations or samples?
questions.detailed_solution:
Analysis of variance is mostly applied when it is necessary to compare more
than 2 populations or samples.
questions.reference: ( Please refer lecture Data Handling and Analytics- Part II @ 9:36)

questions.question: Fill in the blanks. The effect size for determining statistical significance is the standardized _________ difference between two groups.
questions.detailed_solution: The effect size is the standardized mean difference between two groups.
questions.reference: ( Please refer lecture Data Handling and Analytics- Part II @ 17:15)

questions.question: Which of the following is/are some of the future of the IoT applications in the agriculture?
questions.detailed_solution:
Soil moisture monitoring, Automation in vermicomposting, and
Automated weeding system are some of the futures of the IoT applications in the agriculture.
questions.reference: ( Please refer lecture Case study: Agriculture @ 2:15)

questions.question: Which of the following may be considered as the objective of smart water management using IoT?

options.text: Less yield using greater water requirement
Not supporting remote monitoring and controlling

questions.detailed_solution:
Some of the objectives of smart water management using IoT include
more yield using less water, automatic irrigation and remote monitoring and controlling.
questions.reference: ( Please refer lecture Case study: Agriculture @ 4:35).

questions.question: State whether true or false. With the advancement in the sensors and connectivity, it is feasible to enable preventive care along with collecting patient's data.
questions.detailed_solution:
With the advancement in the sensors and connectivity, it is feasible to
enable preventive care along with collecting patient's data
questions.reference: ( Please refer lecture Case study: Healthcare @ 6:30).

questions.question: Integrated design for remote server in AgriSens includes which of the following?
questions.detailed_solution:
Integrated design for remote server in AgriSens includes Web server,
Repository data server and Multi users server
questions.reference: ( Please refer lecture Case study: Agriculture @ 9:16).

questions.question: State whether true or false. The data sensed by the physiological sensors in an IoT-based healthcare system are of no use without proper connectivity and communication.
questions.detailed_solution:
Without proper connectivity and communication, the data sensed by the
physiological sensors are of no use in an IoT-based healthcare system. (Please refer book: S.
Misra, A. Mukherjee, and A. Roy, 2020. Introduction to IoT. Cambridge University Press.
Chapter no.-14, page no.-295)

questions.question: Match the following: Sensor Purpose

options.text: GSR a. Measures the health of a muscle and a nerve cell
EMGc. b. Measures the electrical and muscular activity of the heart
ECGd . c. Measures the intensity of stress on a human
SpO2 d. Measures the pulse and oxygen levels in blood

questions.detailed_solution:
GSR measures the intensity of stress on a human, EMG measures the
health of a muscle and a nerve cell, ECG measures the electrical and muscular activity of the
heart, SpO2 measures the pulse and oxygen levels in blood. (Please refer book: S. Misra, A.
Mukherjee, and A. Roy, 2020. Introduction to IoT. Cambridge University Press. Chapter no.-
14, page no.-296)

questions.question: What is/are the risk/s involved in healthcare IoT?

options.text: Security (e.g., data tampering and unauthorized access)

questions.detailed_solution:
The risk involved in healthcare IoT includes loss of connectivity, security
and error. (Please refer book: S. Misra, A. Mukherjee, and A. Roy, 2020. Introduction to IoT.
Cambridge University Press. Chapter no.-14, page no.-300)

questions.question: State whether true or false. Activity monitoring, particularly in IoT scenario, plays a vital role for rendering better quality of life and safe guarding humans.
questions.detailed_solution:
Particularly in IoT scenario, activity monitoring plays an important role for
rendering better quality of life and safe guarding humans.
questions.reference: ( Please refer lecture Activity monitoring Case Study - I @ 10:00).

questions.question: Fill in the blanks. Processing the sensed data on the device itself is known as __________ approach.
questions.detailed_solution: Processing the sensed data on the device itself is known as in-place approach.
questions.reference: ( Please refer lecture Activity monitoring Case Study - I @ 20:19).

questions.question: What is/are the different purpose/s that made wearable sensors popular?
questions.detailed_solution:
Wearable sensors have become popular for different purposes which
includes Medical, child-care and elderly-care
questions.reference: ( Please refer lecture Activity monitoring (Case Study - I @ 2:15).

source_name: NPTEL - Introduction to Internet of Things

exam_pattern.practice_scoring_model: 1 mark per question inside this practice app

certification_weightage_hint.course_note: NPTEL course pages show that best-assignment aggregation varies by course, while certification commonly uses 25% assignment and 75% proctored exam weightage.
